meta_title: Understanding Inflations Silent Impact on Savings
meta_description: Learn why nominal interest rates don't always accurately reflect your true purchasing power.

hero.title: The Silent Tax on Your Purchasing Power
hero.subtitle: How inflation covertly erodes your bank account balance while you sleep.

content.text: A 5% savings rate sounds incredible on paper, but if the broader economy experiences a 4% inflation spike, your "real return" collapses into a mere 1%. You barely broke even.
Your nominal rate is the sticker price the bank gave you. Your real rate is determined by the Fisher Equation, subtracting out the insidious bleed of CPI increases.
Inflation destroys savings, creates artificial booms, and redistributes wealth to the leveraged.
content.author: Economic Theory
